strategy_outcomes.name: ETH 4h Inventory Reset Rotation v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: price flushes through short-term value, weak inventory is cleared, value is reclaimed, and price rotates back through balance in a pure chop regime
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: The current evidence says dense reclaim/reset mechanics are still the least-broken direction, and ETH 4h remains the cleanest chop-owned lane in the allowed basket. Inventory-reset rotation is structurally clearer and denser than deep continuation or delayed-retest ideas, so it gets primary ownership here.

long: Exit remaining 20% on loss of VWAP or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on reclaim of VWAP or 5 bars

strategy_outcomes.rationale: abort: low trade count (1 < 15) with QS 0.10, PF 10.00, DD 0.0%

strategy_outcomes.name: BTC 4h State Change Hold Expansion v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: directional improvement appears after a reset, fast structure is reclaimed, the reclaim holds on the next bar, and expansion resumes from that improved state
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: The last five cycles still point to transition-friendly control-change structures as one of the few promising directions, but lane fit has to be real. BTC 4h is the better owner here than ETH because it offers more transition character while staying inside the allowed basket. The grammar is deliberately simple: reclaim, one-bar hold, then expansion.
strategy_outcomes.rationale: abort: fail with QS -0.28, PF 0.39, DD 67.4%, trades 191

strategy_outcomes.name: TAO 4h Failed Breakdown Hold Continuation v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: a breakdown attempt fails, support is reclaimed and held, and continuation is entered only once that hold confirms directional reassertion
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: Cycle 89 still needs one continuation-family expression, but the evidence says continuation only belongs where trend contamination is real and the entry is cleaner than breakout theater. TAO 4h remains the only lane where that is true. Failed-breakdown hold keeps the continuation thesis but forces a structurally earlier, more defensible entry.
strategy_outcomes.rationale: abort: low trade count (11 < 15) with QS 0.69, PF 0.72, DD 3.0%

recent_lessons.observation: Strategy QD-20260312-C086-AXS-4H-DEEP-RESET-CONTINUATION-v1 on AXS/4h shows thin edge (PF 10.000) but too few trades (3). Mechanism is too sparse.
recent_lessons.implication: Abandon this sparse mechanism. Do not relax triggers to chase trade count; find a denser concept instead.

guidance.instruction: Work strategy-by-strategy and queue-row-by-queue-row. For each spec, choose exactly one overall decision: pass, refine, or abort. Inside that strategy decision, include one queue_decision for every queue row in the packet. If a strategy produced 0 trades on valid data, treat that as a red flag that must resolve explicitly as either refine or abort. Do not omit any tested row and do not use promote, fix_only, skip, pending, or undecided.
